page: Amazon Web Services c8gn.16xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor and custom AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processors. The instance features 64 dedicated physical cores and 64 vCPUs running at 2.8 GHz, with no simultaneous multithreading, providing one thread per core. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, resulting in a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. The processor cache configuration includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data reveals strong multi-core performance and exceptional memory bandwidth. The instance achieved a stress-ng multi-core score of 109165.81 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 25974.0, both placing in the top tier. Memory bandwidth tests show top-tier results, with cached read-only small block speeds reaching 7395.26 GB/sec and uncached large block write speeds at 212.41 GB/sec. In application-specific benchmarks, the server demonstrated high throughput, achieving 144.9 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and 116.84 GB/sec in static web serving. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1706.69 and a Geekbench text processing score of 2296.0, both falling in the average range.

The c8gn.16xlarge provides a high baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps, making it highly optimized for network-intensive and EBS-optimized workloads. A key tradeoff is the complete absence of local NVMe storage, meaning all storage operations must rely on network-attached EBS volumes. Additionally, while the instance performs strongly in multi-threaded tasks like bzip3 decompression and software compilation, its LLM text generation performance for small models is relatively weak at 71.03 tokens/sec. This server is best suited for high-throughput network applications, multi-threaded compute tasks, software compilation, and high-performance database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor and powered by 64 dedicated ARMv9 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores running at 2.8 GHz. It features 128.0 GB of memory and a 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show top-tier multi-core capabilities, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 25974.0 and a Redis SET throughput of 144.9 million operations per second. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally high, reaching 7395.26 GB/sec for cached reads. However, single-core performance is average, and LLM text generation for small models is weak. This instance is highly cost-efficient for multi-threaded, network-heavy workloads, making it ideal for software compilation, static web serving, and high-throughput database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.16xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance with 64 Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 128 GB RAM, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.16xlarge features 64 ARM64 Graviton cores, 128 GB RAM, and 200 Gbps bandwidth for high-throughput compute workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized platform delivering high-bandwidth networking and exceptional multi-threaded performance for demanding scale-out workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated ARMv9 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores running at 2.8 GHz
128.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
High-speed 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for EBS
Top-tier multi-core performance and memory bandwidth in benchmark testing
No local storage or GPU hardware included
Best suited for software compilation, database operations, and high-throughput web serving
